decision.headline: Pivot away from TIGER as a method-forward contribution and reframe the campaign as a compute-normalized diagnostic benchmark study.
why_not_cand_tiger: Main shortlist failed the core method gate: TIGER tied raw-entropy revision on GSM8K (0.39 vs 0.39).
TIGER beat Prophet and DNB but still trailed CORE-proxy substantially (0.39 vs 0.46).
Boundary gating only partially repaired code degradation and did not beat the Standard code baseline.
why_cand_diag: Held-out calibration produced interpretable diagnostic signal without becoming a persuasive causal method story.
Matched actual compute changed the ranking across Standard, DNB, Prophet, CORE-proxy, entropy revision, and TIGER.
The strongest surviving contribution is understanding when revision helps, when it hurts, and why cheap gating is only a partial fix.

paper_framing.working_title: When Revision Helps Diffusion LMs, When It Hurts, and Why Calibration Correction Is Not Enough
main_claims: Instability-guided revision is not enough by itself to deliver a new method win once strong baselines and honest compute accounting are enforced.
Task gating helps protect code syntax but only partially, and cannot rescue the reasoning story.
The robust contribution is a compute-normalized diagnostic account of revision benefit and failure modes across reasoning and code.
appendix_claims: Syntax guarding is still a useful boundary ablation for code tasks.
Calibration remains diagnostically informative even when it is not the main control signal.

recommended_next_steps: Do not launch math500_transfer for TIGER as a method-scale validation task.
Move the control plane toward synthesis / quality-gate rather than more pilot method scaling.
Treat HumanEval gating as appendix evidence, not the paper headline.
